name: Hexagonal keep silhouette
description: Overall silhouette of a hexagonal keep or hall — an elongated six-sided footprint, full-height across the middle and tapering to a vertical edge at each end. The angled ends deflect attack and give a broader, more imposing presence than a plain rectangle while keeping rectangular rooms in the wide middle bay. Used for castle keeps, chapter houses and fantasy strongholds. Consumed by the global_designer to fix the envelope before space_planner subdivides the bays.
placement_rules: Footprint is an elongated hexagon: full depth in the middle, tapering to a vertical edge at each X-end.
Rectangular rooms occupy the wide central bay; the tapered ends are thick wall, stairs or alcoves.
2-4 storeys suit a keep; a single stair core serves them.
The entrance pierces one of the long flat faces.
Keep the central bay >= 4 deep so rooms fit.

medieval.notes: Rubble-and-ashlar keep with battered hexagonal base and crenellated top.

gothic.notes: Chapter-house hexagon with tall traceried windows on each face.
